tools.name: lightning_network
tools.description:
Bitcoin LIGHTNING NETWORK aggregate statistics — network infrastructure metrics only,
NO price data. Returns node count, public channel count, total public channel capacity
(in BTC and satoshis), average + median channel capacity, the Tor / clearnet / unannounced
node split, and the average fee rate

tools.name: ots_calendar_health
tools.description:
Live health of the public OpenTimestamps (OTS) timestamp-anchoring calendar servers — the
aggregation-and-anchoring infrastructure behind tamper-evident, independently verifiable,
Bitcoin-anchored timestamps. Answers "are the public timestamp calendars up and still anchoring
right now?" before an ag

tools.name: trader_positioning
tools.description:
Who is actually positioned which way in a US futures market — from the regulator itself. The
CFTC Commitments of Traders (COT) report, published every Friday 3:30 p.m. ET, showing the long /
short / spreading positions held as of the prior Tuesday broken out by trader category, plus the
number of re

tools.name: marine_forecast
tools.description:
Marine / surf forecast for a coastal point — current sea state + next-24-hour outlook.
Returns total significant wave height / period / direction, the swell component (long-period
energy driving surf), the locally-generated wind-wave, and sea-surface temperature, plus a
24-hour wave-height band. `lo

tools.name: ocean_buoys
tools.description:
Live marine observations from NOAA NDBC ocean buoys — significant wave height & period,
wave direction, wind speed/direction/gust, barometric pressure (+ 3-hour tendency), air &
water temperature, dewpoint and visibility, each with the buoy's own observation timestamp +
age. `station` = an NDBC stat

tools.name: research_papers
tools.description:
Latest scholarly preprints from arXiv — newest-first — by category and/or keyword.
Returns up to 15 papers, each with: title, authors, truncated abstract, primary + all
categories, published/updated dates, arXiv id, abstract URL, PDF URL, and DOI / journal
reference when a published version exists.

tools.name: species_occurrences
tools.description:
Recent species occurrence records from GBIF (Global Biodiversity Information Facility) —
individual field observations and museum specimens of where/when a species has been recorded.
Each record: species + scientific name, lat/lon, date, country, source dataset (+ GBIF
citation), recorder, and basis

tools.name: public_holidays
tools.description:
Public / statutory holidays for a country (and optional state/province/region) in a given
year — computed in-house, NOT scraped, so it's exact and offline-stable. Per holiday: date,
name, weekday, whether it's an observed-day shift, the country, and the subdivision (when one
was requested), plus the

tools.name: wikidata_facts
tools.description:
Structured factual statements about a named entity from Wikidata — the collaboratively-curated
knowledge base behind Wikipedia. Resolves `entity` to its Wikidata item, then returns the
headline facts: description, instance/class, key properties (e.g. for a country: capital,
population, area, head of

tools.name: health_surveillance
tools.description:
US public-health surveillance signals from the CDC — the current level + week-over-week
direction of circulating illness, not individual case data. `topic` = "respiratory" (default —
emergency-department visit shares + clinical-lab test positivity for COVID-19 / influenza /
RSV) | "wastewater" (CDC

tools.name: clinical_trials
tools.description:
Live clinical-trial registrations and status updates from ClinicalTrials.gov (US NIH/NLM
registry). Returns the most-recently-updated trials, each with: NCT id, title, status
(recruiting / completed / terminated / ...), phase, conditions, lead sponsor (+ class), study
type, enrollment, start / prima

tools.name: uk_water
tools.description:
Live UK river & water levels and flood warnings from the Environment Agency (England) —
real-time gauge readings near a place, each with current level, recent trend (rising / steady /
falling), the station's typical range, plus any active EA flood alerts/warnings for the area.
`location` = a UK plac

tools.name: global_indicator
tools.description:
A flagship development statistic from Our World in Data: the latest value for a country plus
a short multi-year trend, with full source attribution. ONE source, MANY indicators (breadth) —
CO2 per capita, population, fertility, urbanisation, GDP-per-capita (a development stat in PPP,
NOT a market pr

tools.name: pollen_forecast
tools.description:
Airborne pollen / allergen forecast — six pollen types (alder, birch, grass, mugwort, olive,
ragweed) in grains/m³, each with a plain-English severity band (low / moderate / high / very
high), for a hay-fever / asthma / rhinitis audience. Returns the current hour's reading plus the
NEXT-DAY peak per

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "air_quality_fusion" stacks urgency/override words (never, override). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Cross-validated air quality — TWO independent networks reading the same sky: the Open-Meteo CAMS model (the existing air_quality source) vs real ground monitors…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "au_water" stacks urgency/override words (override, warning). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Australian river heights + gauge rainfall near any AU location — live CC-BY-licensed gauge observations from the Bureau of Meteorology's Water Data Online (keyl…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "flood_forecast" stacks urgency/override words (never, override, warning). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: 5-day river-discharge FORECAST near an Australian location — Copernicus CEMS Global Flood Awareness System (GloFAS, operational LISFLOOD control run), retrieved…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: airport_delays, world_cup, security_advisories, reality_check, cite, scholarly_search, bitcoin_network, ots_calendar_health, global_indicator · Private data: search_ai_models, federal_register, software_version, security_advisories, gov_contracts, dns_lookup, scholarly_search, fda_recalls, ots_calendar_health, trader_positioning, ocean_buoys, species_occurrences, clinical_trials · Public sink: airport_delays, emissions, federal_register, world_cup, treasury_yields, spacex, china_data, flood_forecast, bitcoin_network · Destructive: internet_outages, flood_forecast, ocean_buoys, global_indicator.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: airport_delays, world_cup, security_advisories, reality_check, cite, scholarly_search, bitcoin_network, ots_calendar_health, global_indicator · Private data: search_ai_models, federal_register, software_version, security_advisories, gov_contracts, dns_lookup, scholarly_search, fda_recalls, ots_calendar_health, trader_positioning, ocean_buoys, species_occurrences, clinical_trials · Public sink: airport_delays, emissions, federal_register, world_cup, treasury_yields, spacex, china_data, flood_forecast, bitcoin_network · Destructive: internet_outages, flood_forecast, ocean_buoys, global_indicator

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
